prs.title: [WIP] Add TRITON_MLA_SPARSE backend for SM80 sparse MLA support
prs.author: haosdent
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Closes #38006. Enables sparse MLA models (GLM-5, DeepSeek-V3.2) on SM80 (A100/A800) and SM121 (GB10/DGX Spark), where DeepGEMM / FlashMLA-Sparse / FlashInfer-MLA-Sparse are unavailable.

## Changes

1. **Dispatch guard.** `has_deep_gemm()` → `is_deep_gemm_supported()` (SM90+ arch check) in `sparse_attn_indexer.py` and `indexer.py`. Stops DeepGEMM SM90+ kernels from being invoked on SM80/SM121 when the package is installed.
2. **Triton `fp8_mqa_logits` for the indexer.** `mqa_logits_triton.py` reproduces DeepGEMM's prefill + paged MQA logits with a uint8/int bit-trick (`_decode_e4m3fn`) so the kernel compiles on SM80 without `tl.float8e4nv`. Matches the torch reference to bf16 ULPs.
3. **`TRITON_MLA_SPARSE` backend.** `triton_sparse_mla_kernel.py` adds a split-KV decode path with an N-way online-softmax merge, plus a single-pass fast path. Autotune over `(num_warps, num_stages) × (index_topk, NUM_KV_SPLITS, kv_group_num)`; warmed at backend init so no sweep cost in the first request. Masked-out sentinel is `-1e30` (not `-inf`) to avoid `NaN` from `(-inf) − (-inf)` when a whole BLOCK_N tile is masked on short prefills.
4. **Cudagraph support.** `TritonMLASparseMetadataBuilder` (new subclass of the XPU builder) advertises `AttentionCGSupport.UNIFORM_BATCH`; XPU's own builder stays at `NEVER`. On A100 TP=8 this flipped `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` back on and collapsed the per-layer Python-loop overhead.

Layer 1 of #38006 (the C++ `dsv3_fused_a_gemm` kernel) is already handled upstream — `CMakeLists.txt` builds it for SM90+ only, with a `torch.nn.functional.linear` fallback.

## Benchmarks

Kernel (A30, SM80, 56 SMs):

| shape (nt, topk, H) | XPU kernel | this PR | speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1, 2048, 128 (decode) | 0.297 ms | 0.094 ms | **3.15×** |
| 1, 4096, 128 | 0.580 ms | 0.167 ms | 3.46× |
| 1, 4096, 16 | 0.582 ms | 0.081 ms | 7.17× |
| 128, 2048, 128 (prefill) | 4.912 ms | 4.872 ms | 1.01× |

Decode across all (topk, H): median **4.30×**. Prefill: no regre

prs.title: [Fix] Resolve docker build-time LegacyKeyValueFormat warnings
prs.author: bai
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Fix build-time docker env var format warnings:

```
 - LegacyKeyValueFormat: "ENV key=value" should be used instead of legacy "ENV key value" format (line 794)
```

`ENV KEY=value` is supported since Docker 1.4.0 (2014) so backwards compatibility is not a concern.

## Test Plan

Images built and confirmed working in a testbed setup.

prs.title: [ROCm] Use quant_dtype in per_token_quant instead of hardcoded FP8
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`_rocm_aiter_per_token_quant_impl` and its fake both accept `quant_dtype` (which can be `torch.int8` or `FP8_DTYPE` per the assert), but hardcode `FP8_DTYPE` for the output tensor allocation. This means passing `torch.int8` would still get an FP8 tensor back.

Simple fix: use `quant_dtype` instead of `FP8_DTYPE` for `torch.empty()`.

Currently all callers pass `FP8_DTYPE` so this hasn't surfaced, but it's a correctness bug if int8 quantization is ever enabled on this path.

No duplicate PRs found (PR #36092 covers other _aiter_ops bugs but not this one). AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [vllm IR] Port FP8 Quantization to vLLM IR Ops
prs.author: BadrBasowid
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Ports quant fp8 ops to IR following #38745 . The table below tracks all quant fp8 IR ops and their provider implementations.
✅ : Ported
❌: No existing implementation

  | Op | native | aiter | triton | vllm_c |                                         
  |---|---|---|---|---|                                                             
  | `static_quant_fp8` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅|                                        
  | `dynamic_quant_fp8` | ✅ | ✅  | ❌ | ✅ |              
  | `dynamic_group_quant_fp8` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
  | `static_group_quant_fp8` | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |              


### Notes:     
  - per_token_group_quant_fp8_packed_for_deepgemm: Has different semantics from `dynamic_group_quant_fp8` and requires a separate IR op.                                           
  - per_token_group_quant_fp8 : not removed because deep_gemm_moe.py calls it
  with a pre-allocated out_q output buffer. IR ops must be purely functional and cannot write 
  into caller-provided output tensors, so this usage cannot be expressed as an IR op without
  refactoring the call site.

## Follow ups

- [ ] Aiter dynamic quant pattern: Subclass RMSNormDynamicQuantPattern once it's refactored to use VllmFusionPatternMatcherPass to eliminate the duplicate.
- [ ]  Aiter group quant pattern: Same as above for RMSNormGroupQuantPattern.                                                                                         
- [ ] deep_gemm_moe: Replace the per-token group quant call in fp8_utils.py with a direct IR op.

## Test Plan

## Test Result

prs.title: [ROCM] [FEAT] Integrate Aiter hipBLASLt GEMM online tuning
prs.author: hanlin12-AMD
prs.body_head:
Enable ROCm AITER hipBLASLt online tuning via a vLLM env var, and add ROCm tests covering the online-tuning flow and kernel gating behavior.

## Purpose
This PR adds support for enabling hipBLASLt online tuning in vLLM through `VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_HIP_ONLINE_TUNING`, which forwards to `HIP_ONLINE_TUNING=1` early in ROCm platform initialization so tuning is available before hipBLASLt is initialized.
This PR also adds ROCm/AITER test coverage for:
- heuristic `hipb_mm` execution
- explicit solution selection
- CSV cache population for tuned shapes
- forwarding from `VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_HIP_ONLINE_TUNING` to `HIP_ONLINE_TUNING`
- FP8 row-wise scaled GEMM behavior
- force-flag gating for the AITER hipb_mm linear kernel

## Test Plan
- `VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_HIP_ONLINE_TUNING=1 pytest tests/rocm/aiter/test_aiter_online_tuning.py -v`
- `HIP_ONLINE_TUNING=1 pytest tests/rocm/aiter/test_aiter_online_tuning.py -v`

## Test Result
Local test execution was not run as part of drafting this PR description.

Documentation update is not required for this change.

---
